sections.paragraph:
ἐξαστράπτω (s. ἀστράπτω; Pollux 1, 117; Zosimus the alchemist 111 Berthelot; Rhet. Gr. I 640, 31; Tryphiodorus [V a.d.] vs. 103 [ed. Weinberger 1896]; Leontius 44b p. 91, 12; Na 3:3; Ezk 1:4, 7; TestSol 21, 2; TestAbr A 90, 22 [Stone p. 28]; TestJob 46:8; JosAs 22 cod. A [p. 73, 2 Bat.]; GrBar 7:4) flash/gleam like lightning fig. of a white garment Lk 9:29.—DELG s.v. ἀστεροπή (cp. ἀστήρ and ὀπή ‘opening, hole’). M-M. TW.

Pollux Pollux , II a.d.—List 5

Zosimus Zosimus = Z. alchem, III/IV a.d.—List 5

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

Leontius Leontius , Life of St. John the Merciful; VII a.d.—List 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 346
